etymology.description: semantic 言(言) (yán, "word; speak") + phonetic 斥(斥) (chì, "accuse, blame") -> to accuse verbally
etymology.interpretation: Using words to outline an accusation or grievance is the notion described by 言(言) (yán, "word; speak"), while 斥(斥) (chì, "accuse, blame") provides the sound and relates to the action of expressing disapproval or blame through narration.
etymology.simplification: The character was simplified by changing 訁(言) to the shorter radical 讠(言).

examples.chinese: 他向我诉说他的烦恼(他向我訴說他的煩惱)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiàng wǒ sùshuō tā de fánnǎo.
examples.english: He narrated his troubles to me.

examples.chinese: 我的朋友常常与我诉苦(我的朋友常常與我訴苦)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de péngyǒu chángcháng yǔ wǒ sùkǔ.
examples.english: My friend often narrates his complaints to me.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢诉说旅行时的故事(她喜歡訴說旅行時的故事)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān sùshuō lǚxíng shí de gùshì.
examples.english: She likes to narrate stories from her travels.
